headline: In the DRC's Ebola epicenter, a 'cascade of deaths' and fear of contamination
description: Le Monde traveled to Mongbwalu, a town in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, where the Ebola virus outbreak began. As the death toll continues to rise, local health facilities and NGOs have become overwhelmed, hampered by a shortage of resources.

headline: US Supreme Court gives Trump the power to fire top officials across the administration – except at the Fed
Paraguay shock Germany as Brazil, Morocco advance at World Cup
France passes watered-down legislation to curb fast fashion
description: The law aimed at 'reducing the environmental impact of the textile industry' passed its final reading in the Sénat on June 29. Its content underwent numerous revisions over the past two years, particularly to accommodate domestic players in France, while excluding online giants Shein and Temu from those concessions.

headline: 'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri, offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.

headline: Six Bad Bunny songs to know before his concerts in France
description: The Puerto Rican superstar is set to perform at the Stade-Vélodrome in Marseille on July 1 and at the Plenitude Arena in Nanterre on July 4 and 5.

headline: 'The heatwave shows, beyond any doubt, that without strong mitigation measures, adaptation will be impossible'
description: Efforts to combat climate change require far more than mere technical adjustments, writes Le Monde's Stéphane Foucart in his column.

The killing of West Bank children by Israel's army, a product of Palestinians' dehumanization
description: The Israeli human rights organization B'Tselem documented the deaths of 54 minors killed by the Israeli army in the occupied West Bank in January 2025 and the impunity enjoyed by its soldiers.

headline: 'The King and the Mockingbird': The animated masterpiece that inspired Studio Ghibli
description: His film took more than three decades to reach the screen. In 1946, Paul Grimault, then 41, began work on 'Le Roi et l'Oiseau' ('The King and the Mockingbird') with Jacques Prévert writing the screenplay. He finally saw it screened in 1979, at 74. The hand-drawn animation became a cult classic, inspiring generations of viewers and filmmakers.

headline: At the Four Seasons in Damascus, power shifts but the elite remain
description: A symbol of Bashar al-Assad's grip on Syria, the luxury hotel was the scene of lavish parties and business dealings until the fall of the dictator in December 2024. After briefly serving as the headquarters for the new leaders, it is now a networking hub for entrepreneurs seeking business opportunities in a country where everything must be rebuilt.
